steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content: To convert a radian measure to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$. The $\pi$ terms will cancel, leaving a degree measure.

steps.title: Apply the radians-to-degrees conversion formula
steps.content:
To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $$\frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$$. Set up the calculation with the given angle of $\frac{7\pi}{20}$ radians:
$$\frac{7\pi}{20} \times \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$$

steps.title: Cancel pi and simplify
steps.content:
The $\pi$ terms cancel in the numerator and denominator:
$$\frac{7 \times 180^{\circ}}{20}$$
Compute the numerator and divide:
$$\frac{1260^{\circ}}{20} = 63^{\circ}$$
The angle measures 63 degrees.
